title: Calls to .call() and .apply() methods should not be redundant
description: If the function call can be written in a normal way, then calling that function with .call() or .apply() methods is redundant and can be removed without affecting the behavior of the code. The .call() and .apply() methods are traditionally used to explicitely set the value of this keyword when executing a function or an object method. When calling a method of an object the value of this by default will be the reference to that object.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

noncompliant: myFunction.call(null, arg1, arg2); // Redundant when this is not needed
obj.method.call(obj, param); // Redundant when calling on same object
func.apply(null, [arg1, arg2]); // Redundant when this is not needed

checks.message: Remove redundant .call() or .apply() usage when normal function call is sufficient
